setting.note: Two Karachi childhood friends, Raheen and Karim, grow up in the privileged seaside world of Clifton, until Karim's obsession with mapping the divided, violent city and a buried secret from the 1971 war drive them apart. Shamsie maps the real Karachi: the affluent villas of Clifton, the beach at Sea View, the tense old-city districts around Saddar where the ethnic violence flares, and the wider fractured metropolis Karim tries to chart, its wound reaching back to the war that made Bangladesh.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Kamila Shamsie, first published 2002. Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Kartography, Kamila Shamsie (2002). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The blue of the Karachi sea and of the maps through which Karim tries to hold the fracturing city together. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
